UpdateProfileRequest.description: Patch the profile; both fields optional (at least one should be sent).
UpdateProfileRequest.title: UpdateProfileRequest

UserMe.description:
The signed-in user's own view, including profile + preferences (spec 59).

NOTE (spec 68 #22): ``GET /api/v1/users/me`` returns this model — a strict
superset of the spec-08 ``UserPublic`` (adds ``avatar_url``,
``editor_preferences``, ``pending_email``). This is intentional over-delivery
introduced by spec 59; the extra fields never break a ``UserPublic`` consumer.
UserMe.title: UserMe

UserPublic.description: Safe public representation of a user — never includes the password hash.
UserPublic.title: UserPublic

get.summary: Whether a first admin is still needed

schema.title: Response Admin Ping Api V1 Admin Ping Get

post.description:
Send a password-reset link — non-enumerating (same response either way).

Only enqueues the email when a matching user exists; the response is identical
whether or not the address is registered, so it can't be used to probe accounts.
Authenticate credentials and issue a new token family.
post.summary: Log in and receive an access + refresh token pair

post.description: Idempotently revoke the token's family; unknown tokens still return 200.
post.summary: Revoke a refresh token (and its family)

post.description: Rotate the presented refresh token; detect and punish reuse.
post.summary: Rotate a refresh token for a new access + refresh pair

post.description: Create an account from an email, password and display name.

get.description: Always-cheap liveness check; never touches Redis or the database.
Liveness: 200 as long as the process is up; never touches deps.

Readiness check (spec 02 §5.2): Redis-only.

Spec 02 scopes readiness to Redis (no DB exists yet at that point), so this
probe answers ``200`` with ``{"status":"ready","checks":{"redis":"ok"}}``
when Redis is reachable and ``503`` otherwise. The DB-aware readiness probe
is ``/readyz`` (spec 51); do not add a DB check here.
Readiness: 200 when DB + Redis answer within the per-check timeout, else 503.
